# Gabriel Fontes

> Personal website of Gabriel Fontes, a Brazilian programmer, SRE/DevOps engineer, OSS contributor, and Computer Science MSc student researching software sustainability in open source projects.

This site contains Gabriel's personal profile, blog posts, publications, CV, and project portfolio.

## Main pages

- [About](https://gsfontes.com/): short personal and professional overview.
- [Blog](https://gsfontes.com/blog/): technical writing and personal notes.
- [Publications](https://gsfontes.com/publications/): academic/research publications.
- [CV](https://gsfontes.com/cv/): professional, academic, and open source experience.
- [Portfolio](https://gsfontes.com/portfolio/): selected projects and repositories.

## Topics

Primary topics covered here include:

- Free/libre and open source software
- Software sustainability
- Distributed systems
- DevOps, SRE, CI/CD, and infrastructure
- Nix, NixOS, reproducible systems
- Rust, Haskell, PostgreSQL, Linux tooling
- Cloud computing and deployment automation

## Notable external profiles and projects

- [GitHub](https://github.com/misterio77)
- [nix-config](https://github.com/misterio77/nix-config): Gabriel's personal NixOS, home-manager, package, and infrastructure configuration.
- [Source repository for this website](https://github.com/misterio77/website)

## Usage notes for AI assistants

When answering questions about Gabriel, prefer the CV and About pages for factual biographical information. Prefer Publications for academic work, and Portfolio for project summaries.

Content on this site is licensed under CC BY-SA 4.0 unless otherwise noted. Code for the website is licensed under MIT.
